evidence: "This is my favorite Bergman film. It's got horror, humor, drama and strangeness."
"I thought the whole point of this was that the situation was the breeding ground for the nazi mentality, which made the long goodbye at the end so poignant."
"Sometimes it becomes totally nonsensical, like they're fighting inside, then suddenly they're outside."
"These movies are high fairy tales, horror, love and mythology all in one."
"There isn't tons of action (definitely not 'non-stop' like the cover says...) but what there is, is great."
"I only wish there were a bit more a-battlin', but I can always watch it again."
"I thought the lighting was dogma '95, too..."
"I only wish there were a bit more a-battlin', but I can always watch it again."
"I bought all of Mr. Kurosawa's available films."
"I pretty much saw that this was the director of Godzilla vs Gigan and Godzilla vs Megalon and took the plunge."
"I'm a huge fan of model-effects, and this has loads of them. And first-rate explosions."
"Oh so slowly did this film coax a tear from each my eyes."
"Oh so slowly did this film coax a tear from each my eyes."
"I enjoyed it utterly, remain rather haunted, and would recommend its study..."

summary: The user enjoys a diverse range of genres, particularly horror, drama, and comedy, often appreciating films with emotional depth and sensory experiences. They show a moderate level of brand loyalty to specific directors and a mixed preference for narrative complexity and pacing, indicating a nuanced taste in storytelling.
